title: RBAC roles, bindings, and aggregation
description: Roles/ClusterRoles grant verbs on resources; RoleBindings/ClusterRoleBindings attach them to subjects; permissions are additive with no deny.
body: Use when designing or reviewing access. A Role is namespace-scoped; a ClusterRole is cluster-wide or reusable across namespaces. A RoleBinding attaches a Role (or ClusterRole) to subjects within a namespace; a ClusterRoleBinding grants cluster-wide. RBAC is purely additive: there is no deny rule, so effective permission is the union of all bindings and wildcards expand silently. Do not rely on a 'deny' that does not exist or assume a narrow binding offsets a broad ClusterRoleBinding. Actions: enumerate bindings, prefer namespaced Roles, avoid wildcards, use kubectl auth can-i to verify. Validation signals include can-i reflecting only intended access. Risk boundary: a broad ClusterRoleBinding (e.g., to system:authenticated) is a privilege-escalation risk and a blocking finding.
